([Portuguese](/wiki/Portuguese_language "Portuguese language"): *Museu do Amanhã*) is a [science museum](/wiki/Science_museum "Science museum") in the city of [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), Brazil. It was designed by Spanish [neofuturistic](/wiki/Neofuturistic "Neofuturistic") architect [Santiago Calatrava](/wiki/Santiago_Calatrava "Santiago Calatrava"), and built next to the waterfront at Pier Maua. Its construction was supported by the Roberto Marinho Foundation and cost approximately 230 million [reais](/wiki/Brazilian_real "Brazilian real"). The building was opened on 17 December 2015, with President [Dilma Rousseff](/wiki/Dilma_Rousseff "Dilma Rousseff") in attendance.[[1]](#cite_note-Guardian-1)

The **Museum of Tomorrow** ([Portuguese](/wiki/Portuguese_language "Portuguese language"): *Museu do Amanhã*) is a [science museum](/wiki/Science_museum "Science museum") in the city of [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), Brazil. It was designed by Spanish [neofuturistic](/wiki/Neofuturistic "Neofuturistic") architect [Santiago Calatrava](/wiki/Santiago_Calatrava "Santiago Calatrava"), and built next to the waterfront at Pier Maua. Its construction was supported by the Roberto Marinho Foundation and cost approximately 230 million [reais](/wiki/Brazilian_real "Brazilian real"). The building was opened on 17 December 2015, with President [Dilma Rousseff](/wiki/Dilma_Rousseff "Dilma Rousseff") in attendance.[[1]](#cite_note-Guardian-1)

One of the goals in building the museum was to strengthen the cultural and international identity of the city of Rio de Janeiro.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) The Museum was presented as an icon of the reurbanization of the port area.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Exhibitions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Museum_of_Tomorrow&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Exhibitions")]

The main exhibition takes visitors through five main areas: [Cosmos](/wiki/Cosmos "Cosmos"), [Earth](/wiki/Earth "Earth"), [Anthropocene](/wiki/Anthropocene "Anthropocene"), Tomorrow, and Us via a number of experiments and experiences. The museum mixes science with an innovative design to focus on sustainable cities and an ecological world.[[1]](#cite_note-Guardian-1)

The museum was part of the city's port area renewal for the [2016 Summer Olympics](/wiki/2016_Summer_Olympics "2016 Summer Olympics").[[4]](#cite_note-wash-4)
